reasoning: The NFCI has shown 7 consecutive weeks of consistent increases (tightening) from -0.560 on Jan 23 to -0.469 on Mar 13. The week-over-week changes have been monotonically positive and accelerating/stabilizing (+0.004, +0.007, +0.012, +0.015, +0.017, +0.018, +0.018). Given the high positive autocorrelation in the first differences of this dynamic factor model, it is highly likely that the index will increase again in the upcoming week (ending Mar 20, corresponding to the Mar 22 resolution date). The FRED statistical tool suggested slightly below 50% based on long-term linear drift, but this ignores the very strong short-term momentum which dominates weekly forecasts for smoothed indicators.
